summary: PR #2572 applied duplicate-driven matching work produced by re-running `table-typer dups`, touching fighter, game-mode, ground, and item files. The bot report records +2020 matched-code bytes, five newly matched functions, and three unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.role: Large item-collision matching cleanup; bot reports `it_802703E8` newly matched and `it_802706D0` improved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802703E8 rewritten with early return, for loops, continues, typed locals

key_files.role: Formatting and local expression reshaping around Kirby special-N/Lw duplicate-adjacent code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Fighter_ChangeMotionState, lb_8000B1CC, ftWalkCommon_800DFCA4 call formatting and conditional brace layout changes

key_files.role: Fighter dynamics duplicate match for `ftCo_8009D81C` using local renaming, PAD_STACK expression, and direct array indexing.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `data` renamed `hat`, `PAD_STACK(8)` changed to `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)`, `bone_id` local introduced

key_files.role: Game-mode timer function `gm_80163B9C` matched by changing loop semantics to accumulate all 0x19 frame counters and fail on missing entries.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `gm_80163B9C` now initializes `total_frames`, loops `i < 0x19`, accumulates `*gmMainLib_8015D6F8`, and returns false on missing entry

key_files.role: Small local-name adjustment in game main library.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pointer local renamed from `qwe` to `temp_r31` in `gmMainLib_8015D984`

key_files.role: Ground animation duplicate match by replacing expanded helper body with existing helper call plus stack padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `grAnime_801C84A4` now calls `grAnime_801C8318(gobj, arg1, arg2)`, adds `PAD_STACK(8)`, and tests `HSD_AObjGetFlags(aobj)`

key_files.role: Ground Kongo match using stack padding and replacing random helper calls with `rand_range` forms.
Small item function improvement reported for Link bomb animation.
Small item function improvement reported for Link hookshot.

what_changed: The author says the work came from re-running `table-typer dups` after more functions had matched.
The bot report records matched code increasing to 69.90%, +0.05%, +2020 bytes.
Five functions reached 100% according to the bot report: `gm_80163B9C`, `it_802703E8`, `ftCo_8009D81C`, `grAnime_801C84A4`, and `grKongo_801D55D8`.
Three additional unmatched items improved according to the bot report: `it_802706D0`, `itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim`, and `it_802A4BFC`.
`it_802703E8` was substantially reshaped from nested while/goto-style control flow into early returns, `for` loops, and `continue` filters while retaining typed item/fighter/hit/hurtbox accesses.
`grAnime_801C84A4` collapsed an expanded animation-search implementation into a call to existing helper `grAnime_801C8318`, plus a `PAD_STACK(8)`.
`gm_80163B9C` changed from returning on the first present timer entry to accumulating frame totals across 0x19 entries and returning false if any queried entry is absent.

smart_moves.text: Re-running duplicate detection after prior match progress exposed additional small but real matching wins across unrelated subsystems.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says Jelle suggested re-running `table-typer dups` now that more functions matched
text_corpus issue_comment: +2020 matched-code bytes and five new matches

smart_moves.text: Replacing duplicated expanded code with an existing helper in `grAnime_801C84A4` reduced source size and reached a bot-reported match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `grAnime_801C84A4` changed from manual `HSD_ForeachAnim` logic to `grAnime_801C8318`

smart_moves.text: The item-collision rewrite used structured loops and early `continue` filters instead of nested generated-looking control flow.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `it_802703E8` early returns when no hurtboxes, iterates fighters/hits/hurtboxes with `for` loops

decomp_lessons.lesson: Duplicate-search tooling can be useful after nearby functions have matched, but the resulting edits still need narrow match evidence per touched symbol.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `table-typer dups` rerun after more functions matched
text_corpus issue_comment: bot lists exact matched and improved symbols

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function duplicates an existing helper's behavior, replacing the expanded body with the helper call can be the matching source form.
Generated-looking nested collision checks may match better as ordinary structured loops with early exits/continues when the repeated access pattern is over fighters, hit capsules, and hurtboxes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `it_802703E8` converted to fighter/hit/hurtbox `for` loops with early `continue` conditions

naming_conventions.name: `aobj` for an `HSD_AObj*` returned by a ground-animation helper
`temp_r31` retained for a pointer local when semantics are not clarified

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use `PAD_STACK` narrowly where needed for matched duplicate shapes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK changes in ftdynamics.c, granime.c, grkongo.c

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Prefer existing helper calls over manually expanded equivalent logic when duplicate matching indicates shared source shape.
Use loop restructuring and early `continue` filters to reduce generated-looking collision code.
examples: `it_802703E8` iterates `HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters`, hit capsules, and item hurtboxes using `for` loops

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Small expression substitutions can matter for near-matches, such as using `rand_range` in place of random-adder helpers in `grKongo_801D55D8`.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported +2020 matched-code bytes, five new matches, and three improvements in unmatched items.
No review comments or formal review records are present in the loaded PR slice.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, changed files, merged status, counts
human_pr_text: author rationale and duplicate-tooling context
raw_diff: concrete excerpts for 7 of 9 changed files, but diff content is truncated before full item-file coverage
notes: The loaded raw diff is truncated, so details for `itlinkbomb.c`, `itlinkhookshot.c`, and later `itcoll.c` hunks are only partially represented by changed-file metadata and bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Duplicate-tool reruns can reveal small match wins after prior progress
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2572, the author reports re-running `table-typer dups` after more functions had matched; the bot then reported +2020 matched-code bytes, five new matches, and three additional improvements. Treat duplicate-tool output as a candidate generator that still needs per-symbol match evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Expanded duplicate bodies may collapse to existing helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: `grAnime_801C84A4` matched by replacing a manually expanded animation-search body with `grAnime_801C8318(gobj, arg1, arg2)`, plus a narrow stack pad, then checking `HSD_AObjGetFlags`.

source_update_candidates.title: Duplicate-search tooling as candidate discovery
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting `table-typer dups` as a candidate-discovery workflow after related functions have matched. PR #2572 suggests it can produce modest real gains, but the output should remain subject to ordinary per-function match verification and review.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says the PR resulted from re-running `table-typer dups`
text_corpus issue_comment: +2020 bytes and listed matches/improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: This is workflow guidance supported by the PR body and bot report, but should be curated before becoming a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `PAD_STACK` use from this PR as a general style rule; the slice shows narrow uses associated with matches, and existing standards require explicit evidence.
Do not infer that `rand_range` should always replace `random_adder` helpers; the evidence is limited to `grKongo_801D55D8` in this PR.
Do not treat `temp_r31` as a preferred semantic name; it appears to be a conservative/matching local name in one small hunk.
